edges.description: RBM10-associated developmental splicing disruption explains the septal heart defect component of the TARP phenotype.
intermediate_mechanisms: RBM10-dependent splicing programs in embryonic cardiac morphogenesis

edges.description: RBM10-associated developmental dysregulation explains persistence of the left superior vena cava in the TARP pathograph.
intermediate_mechanisms: RBM10-dependent splicing programs in embryonic venous development

edges.description: Robin-sequence mandibular underdevelopment is represented downstream of RBM10-mediated developmental splicing dysregulation.
intermediate_mechanisms: RBM10-dependent splicing programs in craniofacial morphogenesis

edges.description: Palatal clefting is represented downstream of the Robin-sequence craniofacial branch of RBM10-mediated developmental dysregulation.
intermediate_mechanisms: RBM10-dependent splicing programs in craniofacial morphogenesis

edges.description: The clubfoot component of TARP syndrome is modeled as a downstream limb-patterning consequence of RBM10-mediated splicing dysregulation.
intermediate_mechanisms: RBM10-dependent splicing programs in limb morphogenesis

edges.description: The expanded TARP phenotype includes posterior fossa/cerebellar malformations, but the precise RBM10-regulated developmental intermediates remain incompletely specified.

nodes.description: Defect in the atrial septum allowing right-to-left shunting of blood, a cardinal feature of TARP syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Abnormal liver function tests and improved survival in a child with splice mutation TARP syndrome.
evidence.snippet: TARP (talipes equinovarus, atrial septal defect (ASD), Robin sequence, persistent left superior vena cava) syndrome is a rare X-linked disorder affecting the RBM10 gene

nodes.description: Cerebellar and vermian hypoplasia are recurrent posterior fossa brain findings in the expanded TARP syndrome phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Expansion of the TARP syndrome phenotype associated with de novo mutations and mosaicism.
evidence.snippet: These include cerebellar vermis hypoplasia, mega cisterna magna, and cerebellar hypoplasia with or without abnormalities of the corpus callosum.

nodes.description: Cleft of the palate, part of the Robin sequence triad in TARP syndrome.

nodes.description: Loss-of-function mutations in RBM10 (RNA Binding Motif Protein 10) impair its role as a U2-associated splicing repressor, leading to widespread dysregulation of alternative splicing across the transcriptome. RBM10 normally promotes cassette exon skipping through interaction with U2 snRNP at intron branch sites on chromatin. Loss of RBM10 function shifts hundreds to thousands of splicing events toward exon inclusion. Key downstream effects include NOTCH pathway de-repression via NUMB missplicing and dysregulation of extracellular matrix and cytoskeletal signaling through isoform changes in VCL, CD44, and TNC. These splicing defects disrupt developmental pathways controlling craniofacial morphogenesis, limb patterning, and cardiac development, leading to the characteristic TARP phenotype.

biological_processes: Regulation of alternative mRNA splicing via spliceosome
